text: An issuer making an initial public offer of specified securities shall satisfy the conditions of this Chapter as on the date of filing of the draft offer document with the Board and also as on the date of 25[filing] the offer document with the Registrar of Companies.
notes: Reference date for both draft and final offer documents.
title: Reference date

text: The lock-in period for the promoters shall be three years from the date of allotment of shares to them.
title: Lock-in period for promoters

text: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), of which not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets.
title: Net tangible assets requirement (part a)

text: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years (of twelve months each), with operating profit in each of these preceding three years.
title: Operating profit requirement

text: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), calculated on a restated and consolidated basis.
title: Net worth requirement

text: if it has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year has been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.
title: Name change requirement

text: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX;
notes: Application for in-principle approval from stock exchange(s).

source.span_hint: it has made an application to one or more

title: Application for in-principle approval

text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued;
notes: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation of specified securities.

source.span_hint: it has entered into an agreement with a depository

title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation

text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document;
notes: All specified securities held by promoters in dematerialised form before offer document filing.

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form

title: All specified securities held by promoters in dematerialised form

text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited;
notes: All existing partly paid-up equity shares fully paid or forfeited.

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up

title: All existing partly paid-up equity shares fully paid or forfeited

text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.
notes: 25% limit of amount raised for general corporate purposes.

source.span_hint: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue

title: Amount for general corporate purposes

text: such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed thirty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.
notes: 35% limit of amount raised for unidentified acquisition or investment targets.

source.span_hint: such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target

title: Amount for unidentified acquisition or investment targets

text: The issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants in an initial public offer subject to the following: (a) the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment;

source.span_hint: The issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants

title: Warrant tenure, price, and forfeiture

text: c) the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;
notes: Warrant exercise price and upfront payment.

source.span_hint: c) the price or formula for determination of exercise price

title: Warrant exercise price and upfront payment

text: 14. (1) The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital:

source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent.

title: Minimum promoters’ contribution

text: Specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged as a collateral security for a loan granted by a scheduled commercial bank or a public financial institution or a systemically important non-banking finance company or a housing finance company, subject to the following:
notes: Conditions for pledging locked-in specified securities by promoters.

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged

title: Lock-in for promoters' shares

text: Specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred:
notes: Conditions for transferring locked-in specified securities by non-promoters.

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in

title: Lock-in for non-promoter shares

text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation  17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred: Provided that the lock-in on such specified securities shall continue for the remaining period with the transferee and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer them till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.
notes: Provisions for transfer of locked-in specified securities; subject to SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition

title: Transfer of locked-in specified securities

text: (2) Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager, the rights, obligations and responsibilities, relating inter alia to disclosures, allotment, refund and underwriting obligations, if any, of each lead manager shall be predetermined and be disclosed in the draft offer document and the offer document as specified in Schedule I.
notes: Rights, obligations and responsibilities of multiple lead managers; must be predetermined and disclosed in draft and final offer documents.

source.span_hint: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager,

title: Rights, obligations and responsibilities of lead manager(s)
